reasoning: Russia has not conducted a nuclear test since 1990, maintaining a strong norm against testing alongside other P5 nations. While Russia withdrew its ratification of the CTBT in 2023 and hawkish analysts have proposed testing to signal resolve, President Putin has stated that there is no technical or political need to test unless the US tests first. A test would severely risk alienating key partners like China and India, and could spark an unwanted arms race. The timeframe until 2026 is relatively short, leaving the probability of such an escalatory move quite low.
